body.u: When I left school in 1978 I started my apprenticeship in plumbing and heating I served it for 4 years and enjoyed it for its duration I dicideddecided to leave the firm where I served the apprenticeship and start afresh in a new place.

I married when I was 22 and fathered 3 children we put down a deposit on a house straight away and lived at that house for the first ten years of our marriage. We travelled a fair bit going to places like France, Holland and Africa for holiday for the first years of our marriage because of cost. I dicideddecided to start a business of my own when I was 35. We bought a shop and it proved quite successful in the long run.

I left school at 16 because I was lucky enough to get an apprenticeship. I would have stayed on school otherwise. Then the job situation was bad 1 in 5 kids who left got a job, a lot stayed on. I made a lot of friends at school but I sought of drifted away when I left I made a lot of new friends at work and had a good time with my mates before I got married.
I intend to retire at 65 and go into partnership with my two sons in my business because I don’t think I could manage on my own anyway it gives them a headstarthead start in life.

Now my retirement is only five years away I intend to take it easy because I think I worked hard during my working life to get where I am now. If I had the chance to start all over again I don’t think I would.
